summary: A good‑quality recording captures a memorable 1995 Albany show with standout Masterpiece, So Many Roads and the Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence despite Jerry’s occasional vocal and guitar slips.
review: The tape is generally clear and listenable, letting the band’s late‑'90 energy shine through. Highlights include a ripping Masterpiece, an inspired So Many Roads, and a soaring Help‑on‑the‑Way → Slipknot! → Franklin’s Tower, while Jerry Garcia’s health shows up in uneven vocals and guitar work on several numbers. Overall it’s a mixed but enjoyable snapshot of the band’s final tour.

ai_rating.rationale: 22 reviews give a clear picture: solid recording quality (≈4 ★) and a show that is both strong and flawed (≈3.5 ★). Weighted combination yields ~3.7 ★.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers note the sound is “pretty good” or “great,” with no major dropout complaints; occasional mention of typical live ambience but overall clear and balanced.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is uneven, but the second set builds momentum with strong segues into Help/Slip/Franklin’s and a powerful closing Black Peter; overall pacing swings between high‑energy peaks and moments of sloppiness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals and guitar are described as emotional yet uneven—struggling on Franklin's, off‑key on Touch, but delivering heartfelt solos on Masterpiece and So Many Roads.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir shines on Black Peter and provides solid rhythm throughout; his playing is praised especially in the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keys) steps in when Jerry falters—cutting him off on Samba—but also adds strong texture, notably during the Space/Watchtower jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum‑>Space segment is called a “snoozefest,” indicating less excitement compared to other parts.
